pi_items.verified_value: Long Creek Water Treatment Plant (claimed in data), but actual facility is Long Creek Wastewater Treatment Plant
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 32 claims 'Long Creek Water Treatment Plant'. Web search confirms actual facility is a WASTEWATER treatment plant, not water treatment. Long Creek WWTP is a real facility operated by Two Rivers Utilities (TRU) in Gastonia/Dallas, NC area (https://gastonianc.gov/component/content/article/408-386-388-tru-wastewater-facilities.html)

pi_items.verified_value: Clearwater Regional Water Authority (fictional name used in task data). Real operator is Two Rivers Utilities (TRU)
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 31 claims 'Clearwater Regional Water Authority'. Web search found no such organization. The real Long Creek facility is operated by Two Rivers Utilities (TRU), created in 2011 from merger of Gastonia and Cramerton wastewater systems (https://gastonianc.gov/component/content/article/408-386-388-tru-wastewater-facilities.html)

pi_items.verified_value: 3000 Long Creek Disposal Plant, Dallas, North Carolina (for real Long Creek WWTP) or similar in Dallas/Gastonia area
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 33 claims '2200 Reservoir Drive, Gastonia, NC 28054'. Web search found no verification of this address for water/wastewater facility. Real Long Creek WWTP is at '3000 Long Creek Disposal Plant, Dallas, North Carolina' according to https://dallas-nc.north-carolina-pages.com/gastonia-wastewater-treatment.html and https://www.deq.nc.gov/nc0020184-long-creek-wwtp

setup_task.sh line 34 claims capacity_mgd: 12. Web search on Long Creek WWTP found 'expanded...in the mid 1990s to 16 million gallons per day' (https://gastonianc.gov/water-sewer-customer-service.html). Plant was 8 MGD in 1970s, then expanded to 16 MGD by mid-1990s. The 12 MGD value is unclear if it represents historical capacity or is arbitrary.
Verified in setup_task.sh line 31: 'Clearwater Regional Water Authority'
Verified in setup_task.sh line 32: 'Long Creek Water Treatment Plant'
Verified in setup_task.sh line 24: 'Preventive Maintenance Schedule — Fiscal Year 2025' and in pm_tasks

pi_items.verified_value: All equipment manufacturers mentioned are real companies: Xylem Flygt (submersible pumps), LMI Milton Roy, Philadelphia Mixers, Evoqua, Leopold/Xylem, Goulds, ProMinent, Trojan Technologies (UV systems), Grundfos, Peerless Pump, Caterpillar
pi_items.source: Web search verified Xylem Flygt as real submersible pump manufacturer (https://www.xylem.com/en-us/brand/flygt/) and Trojan Technologies as real UV disinfection manufacturer (https://www.trojantechnologies.com/en/products/municipal)

privileged_info_summary: This task uses SYNTHETIC data created by the setup script, not real plant data. The data contains 12 equipment assets and 13 preventive maintenance tasks (3 daily, 3 weekly, 3 monthly, 2 quarterly, 2 annual). While the task references the real 'Long Creek' facility in Gastonia, NC, several facts in the created data are inaccurate: (1) the utility name is fictional ('Clearwater Regional Water Authority' instead of the real 'Two Rivers Utilities/TRU'), (2) Long Creek is actually a WASTEWATER treatment plant, not a water treatment plant, and (3) the stated address (2200 Reservoir Drive) does not correspond to the real Long Creek facility. The plant capacity of 12 MGD in the data appears inconsistent with the real facility's 16 MGD capacity achieved in the mid-1990s. All keywords in the metadata (Clearwater, Long Creek, Preventive Maintenance) are present in the generated data. The document structure and content requirements (heading levels, tables, TOC, page numbers) are defined in the task specification and checked by the verifier.
